title: Select ideas with purged race-blocked forward CV, not only mean random fold AUC
summary: Cross-validation literature is explicit that temporal and hierarchical dependence causes optimistic error estimates when ignored, and the new tyre-state paper itself uses rolling-origin evaluation.
rationale: Cross-validation literature is explicit that temporal and hierarchical dependence causes optimistic error estimates when ignored, and the new tyre-state paper itself uses rolling-origin evaluation. In this dataset, laps within a race-driver trajectory are highly dependent, so overly friendly CV can reward brittle features.
implementation_hint: Run a stricter secondary validator: leave-one-race-out by Year-Race, plus forward-year or rolling-origin splits, and optionally purge a few laps around fold boundaries for any sequential features. Rank experiments by worst-fold robustness and gap between standard CV and blocked CV, not just mean local AUC.
expected_effect: Fewer false positives in research iteration and better odds that a small local gain survives leaderboard evaluation.
risk: Scores will look lower and noisier, so it may slow search unless used as a confirmation gate rather than the only metric.
